title: Marathon Technologies Demonstrates 99.999% Uptime with Assured Availability Systems for Windows NT at SuperComm in Atlanta
author: Marathon Technologies Corporation (Business Wire press release); Peter Kastner and Wayne Kernochan (quoted sources)
abstract: Business Wire press release from Marathon Technologies announcing Endurance Assured Availability nonstop systems demonstrated at SuperComm 1999 in Atlanta. Release quotes Peter Kastner and Wayne Kernochan of Aberdeen Group on the emerging fault-tolerant NT market — 'those applications' requiring standards-based Assured Availability technology. Also quotes GartnerGroup's Joe Barkan (VP NT Strategies) positively. Positions Marathon as carrier-grade Intel-based NT five-nines (99.999%) provider with HP partnership.
importance_rationale: Captures Kastner+Kernochan joint Aberdeen positioning on the late-1990s Windows NT high-availability market, a key Aberdeen practice area; also an example of Marathon Technologies press pickup at peak of fault-tolerant NT hype.
relevance_rationale: Marathon Technologies was acquired by Stratus Technologies in 2012; fault-tolerant NT as a category was subsumed by VMware/VM high-availability and cloud redundancy.
prescience_rationale: Aberdeen's bet that fault-tolerant NT for carrier-grade workloads would be a meaningful market was partially correct — the category existed and Stratus/Marathon succeeded — but it was ultimately absorbed by virtualization-based HA (VMware FT, Hyper-V live migration) rather than remaining a standalone hardware/software category.
